context: Tests whether the agent recognises that PyXLL cell formatters involve Excel COM objects via XLCell.to_range() and consults the pywin32-excel-docs skill before writing the COM formatting code — the non-obvious COM entry point that is easy to miss.

checklist.name: pywin32-excel-docs consulted
checklist.description: docs_log.md records that at least one pywin32-excel-docs resource file was read (e.g. resources/formatting.md, resources/range.md, or resources/index.md)

checklist.name: Formatting resource referenced
checklist.description: docs_log.md specifically mentions a pywin32-excel-docs file related to formatting or the Interior object

checklist.name: Interior.Color or Interior.ColorIndex used
checklist.description: formatter.py sets Interior.Color or Interior.ColorIndex to apply the background colour — a documented COM property

checklist.name: No invented COM property names
checklist.description: formatter.py does NOT use non-existent COM properties such as .BackgroundColor, .FillColor, .CellColor, or .Background

checklist.name: PyXLL docs fetched
checklist.description: docs_log.md records that https://www.pyxll.com/llms.txt or at least one pyxll.com URL was fetched

checklist.name: Documented formatter registration
checklist.description: formatter.py registers the formatter using a documented PyXLL mechanism — not a guessed or invented approach

checklist.name: Background cleared for neutral cells
checklist.description: formatter.py handles the third case: cells that are neither >1000 nor negative have their background colour removed using a documented COM approach

checklist.name: COM not obtained via Dispatch
checklist.description: formatter.py does NOT call win32com.client.Dispatch('Excel.Application') to obtain the Excel object — accesses the COM range via the PyXLL-provided cell object instead

checklist.name: Multiple sources in log
checklist.description: docs_log.md contains at least 3 distinct entries, each with a source and a one-line note

checklist.name: Both value conditions present
checklist.description: formatter.py checks both the >1000 condition and the <0 condition as distinct branches
